customerMessage: I think I need to start taking money out of my account. I'm turning 73 this year and someone told me I have to.

retrievedDocs.name: RMD Age Table (SECURE 2.0)
retrievedDocs.excerpt: Under SECURE 2.0, the RMD starting age is 73 for individuals born between 1951-1959. The first RMD must be taken by April 1 of the year following the year you turn 73.

retrievedDocs.name: RMD Deferral Rules for Active Employees
retrievedDocs.excerpt: Active employees who are not 5% owners may defer RMDs past age 73 until the year they retire, if the plan allows. Ownership percentage must be verified.

suggestedResponse: It sounds like you may be approaching your Required Minimum Distribution (RMD) age. To confirm your specific requirements, I need to verify whether you're still employed and your ownership stake in the plan sponsor. Could you confirm your current employment status?
nextAction: ⚠️ Verify before sending — collect employment status and ownership % before confirming RMD obligation.
whyExplanationFallback: The system detected an RMD eligibility inquiry but could only partially match two documents — the RMD Age Table and the Active Employee Deferral Rules — neither of which fully resolves the question without knowing the participant's employment status and ownership percentage. Because confidence fell to 61% (below the 70% threshold), a fallback clarifying response was generated to safely gather the missing context before confirming any RMD obligation.
